retrieved.title: Change of master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Additional master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. Starting from the beginning of the admission period, you will be automatically admitted to the additional degree programme within five working days. Check the status of your admission in u:space > Study overview. If you have not been admitted within this timeframe, please contact the Admission Office via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933).

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

Previous

©

Next

What do you need to study, how does the test day work, and what do you need to keep in mind? We’ve put together all the information you need – so you can successfully get through the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

[Through the entrance exam](/en/through-the-entrance-exam)

Previous

©

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Resumption of the same degree programme
heading_path: General information about the resumption of studies
Deadlines
retrieved.text:
Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 31 October 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027

Our tip: Submit your application as early as possible!

retrieved.title: Change of master programme
retrieved.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the curriculum of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

retrieved.text: a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). - [Information regarding working languages](/en/entrance-exam/transcultural-communication/working-languages/)

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
heading_path: Application period
retrieved.text:
[#### Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)

Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, there is only one application period per academic year

- Academic year 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 4 May 2026

[#### Bachelor programme in Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/)

Application period irrespective of applicant's citizenship

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 4 May 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027

[#### Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)

Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, there is only one application period per academic year

- Academic year 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 7 April 2026

#### Eligible master programmes without selection procedure for graduates of the University of Vienna

Application period for graduates of an eligible bachelor programme at the University of Vienna according to the curriculum, if no qualitative admission requirements or language skills are stipulated in it (in this case the normal application deadline applies).

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 31 October 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027

Special case: Same day admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German)

Ian Ehm

The application period for degree programmes without entrance exam procedure has started for winter semester 2026/27.](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/news/news/detail-eng/news/application-period-for-bachelor-master-and-diploma-programmes/)

[### Entrance exam procedure 2026/27

01.10.2025

retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
The following deadlines apply for EU/EEA/CH citizens and groups of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/tuition-fee/equal-status-with-eueea-citizens/decree-on-certain-groups-of-persons/).

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 5 September 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 5 February 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

The following deadlines apply for citizens of non-EU/EEA countries.

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 3 August 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

reranked.title: Additional master programme
reranked.text:
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- from 2 March to 7 April 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Note the potentially required additional proofs. You can find these in the [curriculum](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/) of your degree programme.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. If you fulfil all requirements you can be admitted to the new degree programme. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)) after a processing time of a few weeks. You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

The Admission Office will inform you about further steps via e-mail.

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure
Note: New curriculum from winter semester 2026/27
reranked.text: a few days in u:space by clicking on Studies > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail. Please note: The admission to the degree programme with the new working languages is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). - [Information regarding working languages](/en/entrance-exam/transcultural-communication/working-languages/)

reranked.title: Application and admission periods
heading_path: Application period
reranked.text:
[#### Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)

Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, there is only one application period per academic year

- Academic year 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 4 May 2026

[#### Bachelor programme in Sport Science](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/sport-science/)

Application period irrespective of applicant's citizenship

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 4 May 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027

[#### Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)

Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, there is only one application period per academic year

- Academic year 2026/27:
  from 2 March to 7 April 2026

#### Eligible master programmes without selection procedure for graduates of the University of Vienna

Application period for graduates of an eligible bachelor programme at the University of Vienna according to the curriculum, if no qualitative admission requirements or language skills are stipulated in it (in this case the normal application deadline applies).

- Winter semester 2026/27:
  from 22 June to 31 October 2026
- Summer semester 2027:
  from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027

Special case: Same day admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German)

reranked.title: Entrance exam procedure
heading_path: News
reranked.text:
[### Apply now for programmes without entrance exam procedure!

23.06.2026
Studying at the University of Vienna

©
Ian Ehm

The application period for degree programmes without entrance exam procedure has started for winter semester 2026/27.](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/contact-form/news/news/detail-eng/news/application-period-for-bachelor-master-and-diploma-programmes/)

[### Entrance exam procedure 2026/27

01.10.2025
Bachelor's programmes with entrance exam

©
Universität Wien

All changes at a glance](/en/news/detail/entrance-exam-procedure-2026-27)

[All news and events](/en/news)

Interested in a master's programme?

##

Find out more about the master's programmes at the University of Vienna now!

[Admission to a master's programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/)

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
heading_path: Further bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
The following deadlines apply for EU/EEA/CH citizens and groups of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/tuition-fee/equal-status-with-eueea-citizens/decree-on-certain-groups-of-persons/).

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 5 September 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 5 February 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

The following deadlines apply for citizens of non-EU/EEA countries.

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 3 August 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

selected.title: Change of master programme
heading_path: Master programmes without entrance exam procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Deadlines
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

[2] Change of bachelor/diploma programmes — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Deadlines
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

[3] Additional bachelor/diploma programme — Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure > Deadlines
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

[4] Studying a non-degree programme (again) — Attending individual courses > Deadlines
Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

[5] Change of master programme — Master programmes without entrance exam procedure > Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

[6] Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme — Different bachelor/diploma programme with entrance exam procedure > Deadlines
Application and admission period for the academic year 2026/27:

- 2 March to 4 May 2026

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Admission period: after the announcement of results to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

Question: What are the application dates for a bachelor or master starting in winter 2026/27?

generate.raw_response: For bachelor programmes with entrance exams, the application period is from 2 March to 4 May 2026 [1][2][3][6]. For master programmes without entrance exams for University of Vienna graduates, the application period is from 22 June to 31 October 2026 [5].

answer: For bachelor programmes with entrance exams, the application period is from 2 March to 4 May 2026 [1][2][3][6]. For master programmes without entrance exams for University of Vienna graduates, the application period is from 22 June to 31 October 2026 [5].
